heroSubtitle: Experience the soul of Trieste. High-quality coffee, exclusively roasted with beechwood according to an ancient 18th-century tradition.
seasonalDescription: A curated selection of blends—each designed as a sensory masterpiece, shaped by flame, time, and the Triestine palate.
tickerText: TRADITIONAL BEECH WOOD ROASTING — ATT CAFFÈ — TRIESTE — TRADITION SINCE 1995
editorialBody: Every coffee is treated with patience and care. Guided by skilled roasters, each batch is watched and listened to-timed to the delicate “first crack” and the subtle color changes that signal true perfection. This is coffee made without rush, where every bean tells a story.
editorialPoint1Body: Hailing from the legendary coffee city where the port meets the palate, this tradition transforms espresso into an art form. Each cup carries a rich heritage, honoring decades of expertise and the culture that perfected it.
editorialPoint2Body: The care extends beyond the cup. By embracing regenerative practices, make certain the land continues to produce exceptional coffee cherries for generations, blending sustainability with excellence in every sip.
woodWhyBody: Beechwood fire roasts the coffee slowly and gently, letting heat reach deep inside each bean. This classic method keeps the bean’s natural oils intact, creating a rich aroma and a flavor you can’t get with modern machines. The result? A cup that’s smooth, balanced, and full of character.
woodCard1Body: Soft, even heat from beechwood preserves the bean’s natural oils and fragrance.
woodCard2Body: After roasting, the coffee rests to develop sweetness, structure, and a creamy, satisfying finish.
sommelierSubtitle: Let our digital connoisseur curate a coffee ritual based on your mood and time of day.
sommelierFallbackDescription: A dark, velvety cup with a persistent cream—excellent for espresso and milk drinks alike.
footerTagline: Authentic Trieste coffee, roasted over beechwood since 1995. Represented in Lithuania.
koliziejusNote: Official ATT Caffè representative in Lithuania. Original Italian products.
b2bDescription: We are eager to collaborate with cafes, restaurants, and companies. We offer favorable terms and individual solutions.
newsletterDescription: Get updates on seasonal blends, limited editions, and coffee rituals from Trieste.
newsletterNote: Newsletter sent once a month. Unsubscribe anytime.
balticsTitle: Authentic Italian Wood Roasted Coffee — Now in the Baltics
balticsBody1: At Koliziejus, an Italian specialty shop in Vilnius, we bring authentic Italian flavors directly to Lithuania and Baltic region.
balticsBody2: In our showroom we proudly represent ATT Caffè in the Baltics, offering B2B supply for cafés, restaurants, hotels, offices, and specialty stores that want to serve authentic Italian espresso.
balticsBullet1: Visit our showroom in Domus Pro - Ukmergės g. 308, Vilnius, Lietuva, LT-12110
balticsBullet3: Wholesale supply across Lithuania and the Baltic region
balticsClosing: Serve real Italian coffee with history and character.
companyDescription: Authentic Trieste coffee roasted over beechwood. Represented in Lithuania by Koliziejus.
aboutIntro: Founded by passionate connoisseurs, Antica Tostatura Triestina honors Trieste’s coffee tradition by crafting high-quality blends through an accurate selection of green coffees and the art of wood roasting—restoring espresso to its original flavor.
philosophyBody: From Trieste, the roastery selects premium beans across the Americas, Africa, and India, then roasts them slowly over beechwood—an artisanal technique born in the 18th century. This commitment to authenticity and research creates blends with unique aromatic complexity.
philosophyPillar1Body: Only the best varieties of green coffee are selected from renowned plantations and tested before roasting—so quality begins long before the flame.
philosophyPillar2Body: A traditional Triestine technique: gentle beechwood heat slowly roasts each bean to its heart, preserving aroma, structure, and natural flavor.
philosophyPillar3Body: Each origin requires its own roasting curve and timing. Roasting per single origin helps each bean reach its highest aromatic expression.
philosophyPillar4Body: The production method meets strict regulatory requirements and certifications, ensuring both organoleptic excellence and food safety.
storySection1Body: Antica Tostatura Triestina has earned a high reputation among acknowledged coffee lovers worldwide. The mission is to honor Trieste’s tradition by crafting high-quality blends through accurate selection and wood roasting—creating intense aromatic notes that awaken deep emotions and recall the sweetest memories.
storySection2Body: Trieste is a beacon of coffee culture: in 1719 its port became a free port, and in 1904 it hosted the world’s first coffee exchange. For three centuries, trade, roasting companies, and literary cafés shaped a unique tradition.
storySection3Body: Founded in 1995, Antica Tostatura Triestina specializes in wood roasting and high-quality blends. From its headquarters in Trieste, the company selects and wood-roasts only the best green coffees—reviving an 18th-century artisanal technique.
storySection4Body: A sensory journey from Central and Latin America through Africa to India. Green coffees are tested before roasting, each origin is roasted slowly at low temperatures, and the process ends with air cooling to preserve aroma and structure.
storySection5Body: This commitment has carried ATT coffee around the world. The New York Times named Antica Tostatura Triestina in the article “New York’s Best Espresso” (NYT – Vol. 52028 – 15/05/2002).
storySection6Body: Wood roasting is delicate and gradual: beechwood heat envelops the bean softly and evenly. Because each origin differs, a dedicated working cycle is developed for every variety—roasting curve, extraction temperature, and time—to valorize the unique organoleptic properties of each bean.
